disclaimer: Under Selection Room's four-factor model, the committee's published top 25 looks more résumé-heavy and less predictive-driven than Selection Room's baseline.
disclaimer_short: These weights are descriptive approximations, not the committee's actual weights. Research-only; they do not change Selection Room's production model.

badge_explainers.Edge-weight fit: Edge-weight fit means one or more factors landed near 0% or very high, so interpret the direction more than the exact number.
badge_explainers.Volatile weekly fit: Fitted weights swing between releases this season; read the direction, not week-to-week magnitudes.

entries.fit_warning: Edge-weight fit: one or more components are near 0% or above 50%; interpret as directional, not exact.; Several nearby blends explain the committee almost equally well, so treat the deltas as directional rather than exact.

interpretation.headline: More résumé-heavy and less predictive-driven than the production baseline
interpretation.warning: Edge-weight fit: one or more components are near 0% or above 50%; interpret as directional, not exact.

explains: The committee's ranking is better approximated by a heavier résumé emphasis than the production baseline.
A lighter predictive, SOR, and SOS emphasis fits the committee's order better than the baseline blend.
The fitted blend reduces mean absolute rank error against the committee from 2.5833 to 1.5.
Teams moved toward their committee position: Marshall, Boise State, Louisville.
does_not_explain: The résumé weight sits at the edge of the searched simplex, so its exact magnitude is directional, not identified.
Several nearby blends fit almost equally well (component spread up to 45pp), so the exact split between components is not identifiable from this season alone.
Rank-error magnitudes are not yet comparable across seasons; treat cross-year fit-quality comparisons as an open question.

public_case_2025.committee_order: Miami #10, Notre Dame #11 (Notre Dame first team out)
public_case_2025.headline: More résumé-heavy and less predictive-driven than the production baseline
public_case_2025.explanation: The fitted blend improves alignment with the committee's top 25 overall but still ranks Notre Dame above Miami, so it does not fully reproduce the committee's Miami-over-Notre-Dame ordering. That suggests this split may depend on factors outside the four-factor model (for example head-to-head context, best wins, or committee-specific judgment) or on a bubble-specific weighting. The fit does narrow the gap: Miami gains 2 positions on Notre Dame relative to the baseline.

caveats: The fitted weights are the closest transparent approximation to the committee's published rankings under Selection Room's four-factor model. They are not the committee's actual weights.
Research mode only: fitted weights never change production defaults.
Fitted weights are descriptive approximations, not normative recommendations and not the committee's secret formula.
Near-optimal regions mean multiple weight blends fit similarly; treat deltas as directional, not exact.
Weekly fits are noisier than final-field fits; early-season shifts are directional signals only.
